sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Current nodes:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['N', 'W', 'c', 'b', 'g']:
        print(f"  {n['color']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Player is at cols 13-15, rows 32-35. White strip is at col 12, rows 32-35. The left gray box is at cols 16-19, rows 28-31.
The player is now just to the LEFT of the left gray box. I need to move RIGHT 1-2 steps to be below the left gray box.
Actually, looking at the ascii image, the player (green) is to the left of the left gray box. The player is at cols 13-15 and the left gray box is at cols 16-19. So the player is adjacent to the left gray box.
Now I should try pressing SPACE to drop the white strip. But wait, the white strip is at col 12 (to the left of the player). When I press SPACE, what happens?
Actually, I think the white strip follows the player. When I drop it (SPACE), it appears below the player. Let me try pressing SPACE now.
But first, let me check: is the player currently carrying the white strip, or is it on the ground? The segmentation shows them as separate objects, so the strip is on the ground.
When the player is not carrying anything and presses SPACE near a gray box, it might try to collect the box. But there's no white strip below the left gray box.
Actually, I think the white strip at col 12 IS the carried item, just rendered separately. Let me try pressing SPACE to drop it.

sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player is at cols 13-15, just left of left gray box (cols 16-19)
# Try pressing SPACE to drop the white strip
action(['SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
